text.title: Architecture Decision Records Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Architecture Decision Records application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: ADRs MUST live in version control next to code; wikis rejected.
rule.rationale: Wiki ADRs lose context-link to the code they explain.

rule.statement: ADR status MUST be one of {Proposed, Accepted, Deprecated, Superseded}; ad-hoc statuses rejected.
rule.rationale: Closed lifecycle keeps ADR readers oriented.

rule.statement: Accepted ADR MUST NOT be edited; supersession creates a new ADR linking back.
rule.rationale: Editing accepted ADRs erases history of why.

rule.statement: ADR MUST list ≥2 alternatives with one-line rationale each.
ADR MUST state ≥1 positive and ≥1 negative consequence.
rule.rationale: All-positive ADRs hide the trade-off being accepted.
